title: Closing Practice-Core portability remediation arc (Rounds 1+2+3)
body: Three foreground rounds completed sequentially after both subagents (observability WS1 + Practice-Core) timed out without committing. Round 1: subagent worker swept .agent/practice-core/** for ADR-NNN references, ../../../docs/architecture/architectural-decisions/... links, ../../skills/ + ../../commands/ + ../../memory/ + ../../plans/ + ../../experience/ external-repo-path links, commit-SHA citations, and re-routed substance through .agent/practice-index.md as the bridge target. Round 2 (foreground): docs-adr-reviewer pass surfaced (a) doctrine-setting clause in decision-records/README.md needed tightening to forbid §Host-local context inline, (b) ~30 §Host-local context / §Host context note sections across PDRs were missed by Round 1, (c) trinity (practice.md / practice-bootstrap.md / practice-lineage.md) still taught the 2026-04-29-retired patterns/ Core directory and practice-context/ peer companion as live, (d) 3 broken stale-name PDR-007 links (PDR-005:9, PDR-032:10, PDR-037:18), (e) PDR-006:226 machine-local path (~/code/personal/algo-experiments) violating the host's own no-machine-local-paths rule, (f) bridge-index mutable counts (43/12/36/77) per moving-targets-in-permanent-docs doctrine, (g) PDR-024:73,332 retired-surface citations, (h) PDR-034:154 host-path leak (apps/...), (i) PDR-004:33 host-repo-name framing. distilled.md amended inline with C6 (Practice-canonical directory references = portable structural contract, not host leakage) and C7 (external http(s) citations to durable third-party material = permitted) carve-outs ratified by owner direction. Round 3 (foreground, this turn): docs-adr re-review issued GO-WITH-CONDITIONS surfacing four further gaps — 3 trinity surfaces still teaching patterns/ as live, PDR-028:135 dangling forward-reference to deleted §Host-local context, PDR-033:48-49,67-68 + PDR-034:27-28 host-repo-name carrier-of-meaning instances, decision-records/README.md:107-109 cross-reference target needed extension. All four cleared. Plus inline-host-anchoring sweep across 11 PDRs (001/003/004/005/013/019/026/027/040/041/042) generalising 'this repo' / 'in this repo' / 'PR-NN' / host-tooling phrases. Plus PDR-034 fixture-string instances (lines 72, 83) abstracted to placeholder shapes. Plus trinity surfaces (practice.md / practice-bootstrap.md / practice-lineage.md / practice-verification.md / CHANGELOG.md body content) generalised. Quality gates after Round 3: portability:check PASS, markdownlint:root PASS, format:root PASS, practice:fitness shows no NEW violations introduced — all four practice-core trinity files remain in soft zone (overall fitness CRITICAL is owned by pre-existing violations in napkin.md / repo-continuity.md / distilled.md / principles.md / pending-graduations.md — out of scope). Three open follow-on items (all captured): (1) structural-enforcement scanner per PDR-038 to prevent recurrence — sketched in napkin.md §Structural enforcement gap; (2) CHANGELOG attribution headers (## [oak-open-curriculum-ecosystem] DATE — TITLE) and provenance.yml host-repo entries retained as structurally-required attribution metadata (analogous to git commit author lines / semver version tags) — candidate distilled.md amendment if borderline-finding recurs; (3) PDR-007 body itself preserved as historical record per PDR convention (legitimately discusses pre-/post-amendment shape per its §Affected sections callout). Active-claims.json never recorded a Practice-Core claim due to a likely race condition with the WS1 worker's parallel write — handled manually by direct comms (open + close events). No claim entry to remove.
